alternates: how to restart a flamed out engine
how to relight a flamed out engine
how do i restart an engine?

source.section: Part 7 - Engine & Fuel Management > Powerplant Systems > Engine Relight Procedure

steps.action: Set flamed out engine throttle to IDLE or above.
steps.voiced: Set the flamed-out engine throttle to IDLE or above.

steps.action: Adjust aircraft airspeed to 350 kts or more. Maintaining a high airspeed will allow windmilling (air flow driving the engine compressor blades) to maintain an engine RPM between 12 % and 45 %, facilitating engine start. Windmill restart attempts made after RPM has degraded to 0 % may require up to 450 kts to obtain 12 % RPM for ignition.
steps.voiced: Accelerate to 350 knots minimum — windmilling holds RPM in the 12 to 45 percent band for ignition. If RPM has decayed to zero, you need 450 knots.

steps.action: Make sure that you are flying below 25000 ft.
Set ENG CRANK Switch to start flamed out engine
steps.voiced: Set ENG CRANK Switch to start the flamed-out engine.

steps.action: In case of a failed engine cross-bleed start, an engine restart with the APU as the bleed air source can be use as the last alternative.
steps.voiced: If cross-bleed start fails, select APU as bleed air source for a last-resort restart.
